content_type: propaganda graphic (enforcement photo with slogan overlay)
description: A designed graphic built from an outdoor enforcement photograph with a slogan overlay. The photo shows two federal agents, seen from behind, escorting a detained man along a brick-walled walkway with green shrubbery. The two officers wear dark navy raid jackets with large light lettering across the back: the left officer's jacket reads 'POLICE / FEDERAL AGENT' and the right officer's reads 'POLICE' with 'HSI' (Homeland Security Investigations) partially visible; the right agent's face is digitally blurred. The man being escorted in the center wears a dark green pullover and tan pants and appears to have his hands restrained behind his back. Overlaid at the lower right is a solid blue rectangle containing white bold serif text 'Making America Safe Again.' The visual style is documentary arrest photography turned into branded messaging via the slogan box, consistent with the recurring USCIS/DHS 'Making America Safe Again' campaign. Messaging technique: showcases a real apprehension (here, an alien linked to suspected human smuggling) as proof of tough enforcement and the USCIS-ICE partnership; the back-of-jacket 'POLICE/FEDERAL AGENT/HSI' lettering and restrained subject convey authority and successful action. Target audience: general public and supporters, projecting strength and deterrence.
notable_text: Making America Safe Again / POLICE FEDERAL AGENT / POLICE HSI
